hero.title: 4 Days: Best of Cambodia
hero.titleAccent: Angkor Temples + Sunset Mekong River Cruise.
hero.intro: Cambodia's two greatest cities in four days. Begin in Siem Reap with the full Angkor temple circuit — Angkor Wat, Angkor Thom, Ta Prohm, and Pre Rup sunset. Then travel to Phnom Penh, the French-colonial riverside capital where the Mekong and Tonle Sap rivers meet. Explore the city's royal and historical sites and end with a golden-hour cruise on the Mekong as Phnom Penh's skyline lights up at dusk.

breadcrumb.label: 4 Days Best of Cambodia & Mekong Cruise

hero.heroImageAlt: Mekong River sunset cruise Phnom Penh Cambodia golden hour

includes: Return airport transfers (Siem Reap arrival, Phnom Penh departure)
Meals as per itinerary (B = Breakfast, L = Lunch, D = Dinner)
All tours and sightseeing as per itinerary
Tourist bus transfer Siem Reap to Phnom Penh
excludes: Travel insurance (ETIQA Area 2 Gold from RM41)
Royal Palace entrance ticket (USD 10, own expense if entering)

overview.heading: 4 Days Cambodia: The Full Picture
body: This package covers the essential Cambodia — both of the country's most significant destinations in a single efficient four-day itinerary. Siem Reap for the temples, Phnom Penh for the capital. Angkor Wat in the morning light, the Mekong at golden hour. The ancient and the modern, the profound and the everyday, all in four days.

Day 2 is the full Angkor Archaeological Park circuit — everything from Angkor Wat itself to Ta Prohm and Pre Rup. Day 3 is the journey south to Phnom Penh: six hours by tourist express bus passing through Cambodian countryside, with a stop at Skuon Market. Arriving in the afternoon, your guide takes you straight to Wat Ounalom — the oldest Buddhist foundation in Cambodia, established in 1443 — before the highlight of the day: a Mekong River sunset cruise departing from the famous riverfront esplanade.

The Mekong cruise is consistently the moment guests describe most vividly when they return home. The boat moves slowly south from the Tonle Sap confluence past Diamond Island, traditional fishing villages, and stilted river communities as the sun drops over the city and Phnom Penh's lights come on. Nothing quite prepares you for how beautiful it is.

highlights.heading: Why This 4-Day Cambodia Package Stands Apart

items.title: Mekong River Sunset Cruise — Phnom Penh's Finest Hour
items.body: A private sunset cruise departing from the Phnom Penh riverfront esplanade, moving south on the Mekong past Diamond Island, traditional fishing villages, and communities of stilted houses over the water. The light changes as the sun drops behind the city. The skyline of Phnom Penh appears in silhouette against an orange and purple sky. The Tonle Sap confluence — where two of Asia's great rivers meet — passes beneath the bow. By the time you return to the dock, the city lights are on and the riverfront restaurants are full. It is the single most cinematic evening in Cambodia.
items.imageAlt: Mekong River sunset cruise Phnom Penh Cambodia golden hour

items.title: Angkor Wat & The Full Temple Circuit
items.body: The world's largest religious monument covered in a full unrushed day. Angkor Wat at sunrise, Angkor Thom's walled royal city, the Bayon with its 200 carved faces, Ta Prohm with its famous tree roots (the Tomb Raider filming location), and Pre Rup for the classic Angkor sunset. Your guide covers the history, the mythology, and the story of the civilisation that built all of this.
items.imageAlt: Angkor Wat temple towers morning light Cambodia

items.title: Wat Ounalom — Cambodia's Oldest Buddhist Foundation
items.body: Founded in 1443, Wat Ounalom is the centre of Cambodian Buddhism and the seat of the country's Buddhist Patriarch. The complex of temples, shrines, and buildings on the Phnom Penh riverfront survived the Khmer Rouge period, though it was badly damaged. Its rebuilding is one of the most significant stories of Cambodian cultural recovery. The Mekong River is visible from the grounds and the atmosphere is one of the most peaceful in the city.
items.imageAlt: Wat Ounalom Phnom Penh Cambodia oldest Buddhist temple

items.title: Skuon Market — The Road Between Two Cities
items.body: The six-hour journey from Siem Reap to Phnom Penh passes through the Cambodian countryside — rice fields, sugar palms, and traditional wooden houses on stilts. The stop at Skuon Market is a genuine Cambodian experience: the market is famous throughout Southeast Asia for its fried tarantulas, a local delicacy eaten with salt, sugar and chilli. Even if you decline the tarantulas, the market itself — the produce stalls, the noise, the energy — is worth the stop.
items.imageAlt: Cambodian countryside rice fields road between Siem Reap and Phnom Penh

days.title: Arrival Siem Reap — City History Tour
days.description: Arrive at Siem Reap International Airport and transfer to hotel. Afternoon city history tour: Wat Preah Prom Rath, Preah Ang Chek and Preah Ang Chorm Shrine (the Sisters Temple), Royal Gardens, Wat Thmei Killing Field Memorial, War Memorial Monument. Visit Cambodia's Best Duty-Free Store. Dinner and overnight in Siem Reap.
suggestedActivities: Siem Reap airport arrival & hotel transfer
Wat Preah Prom Rath city temple
Preah Ang Chek & Preah Ang Chorm Sisters Temple

days.title: Full Day Angkor Archaeological Circuit | Traditional Khmer Dance Dinner
days.description: Full day at the Angkor Archaeological Park. Morning: Angkor Wat at sunrise, the world's largest religious monument. The 350-metre causeway approach, the outer gallery bas-reliefs, and the five central towers. Angkor Thom South Gate causeway; the Bayon temple with 200 carved stone faces; Baphuon pyramid; Terrace of Elephants; Terrace of the Leper King. Afternoon: Ta Prohm (Tomb Raider temple), Banteay Kdei monastery, Srah Srang reservoir, Pre Rup sunset pyramid. Evening: Traditional Khmer Apsara dance buffet dinner (subject to availability). Evening at Pub Street and Night Market.
suggestedActivities: Terrace of Elephants & Terrace of Leper King

days.title: Travel to Phnom Penh | Skuon Market | Wat Ounalom | Mekong Sunset Cruise
days.description: Check out from Siem Reap hotel and depart by tourist express bus for Phnom Penh (approximately 6 hours on the main highway south). En route stop at Skuon Market, famous throughout Cambodia and Southeast Asia for its fried spider snacks and a genuinely authentic local market experience. Arrive Phnom Penh early afternoon. Lunch on arrival. Afternoon: Wat Ounalom — Cambodia's oldest Buddhist foundation (est. 1443), the seat of the country's Buddhist Patriarch, with the Mekong visible from the temple grounds. Evening sunset cruise on the Mekong River: departing from the Phnom Penh riverfront, the boat passes the Tonle Sap confluence, Diamond Island, fishing villages, and stilted communities as Phnom Penh lights up at dusk. Dinner and overnight in Phnom Penh.
suggestedActivities: Tourist express bus Siem Reap to Phnom Penh

days.title: Phnom Penh Discovery Tour — Depart
days.description: Full morning Phnom Penh discovery tour: Central Market (one of Asia's finest French-colonial market buildings, completed 1937), Wat Phnom (hilltop temple where the city was founded in 1372), Daun Penh Statue, Independence Monument, and King Norodom Sihanouk Memorial. Walk the riverfront esplanade past the Royal Palace compound (exterior view). Lunch at city restaurant. Transfer to Phnom Penh International Airport for onward journey.

sections.heading: 4 Day Siem Reap & Phnom Penh Tour Package from Malaysia
sections.body:
A 4-day tour combining Siem Reap and Phnom Penh is the most efficient way to see both sides of Cambodia: the ancient Khmer Empire that built Angkor, and the modern Cambodian capital on the Mekong. Four days is tight but very manageable — Day 2 for the full Angkor circuit, Day 3 for the transfer south and the Mekong cruise, Day 4 for Phnom Penh's highlights before departure.

The tourist express bus between Siem Reap and Phnom Penh takes approximately 6 hours and passes through the heart of the Cambodian countryside — a journey worth doing for what you see out the window. For those with limited time, a domestic flight (40 minutes) can replace the bus journey on request.

sections.heading: The Mekong River Cruise: What to Expect
sections.body:
The Mekong River cruise departs from the Sisowath Quay riverfront in central Phnom Penh, where the esplanade is lined with restaurants and bars that become particularly atmospheric at sunset. The boats head south on the Mekong past Diamond Island (a government-developed recreation area in the middle of the river), Koh Dach (Silk Island, visible in the distance), and a series of fishing villages and stilted river communities.

The cruise typically lasts 90 minutes to 2 hours. The Tonle Sap river confluence — where the Tonle Sap meets the Mekong in one of Southeast Asia's most significant river junctions — is visible from the boat. Most guests describe the returning leg, heading north toward Phnom Penh as the city lights come on, as the most beautiful part of the cruise.

sections.heading: Phnom Penh: Beyond the Main Sites
sections.body:
Most Phnom Penh itineraries focus on the Royal Palace and Toul Sleng. This package adds a morning discovery of the city's French-colonial heritage: the Central Market (Psar Thmei), one of Asia's finest art deco market buildings completed in 1937 with its distinctive central dome and four wings; Wat Phnom, the hilltop temple where the city was founded in the 14th century; and the Independence Monument, built in 1958 to mark independence from France and modelled on the towers of Angkor Wat.

For those who want to add the Royal Palace and Toul Sleng Genocide Museum, we recommend upgrading to the 5-day package which includes both sites with full time to explore them.

items.question: Do Malaysians need a visa for Cambodia?
items.answer: Yes. Malaysian passport holders require a visa to enter Cambodia. Options include the Cambodia e-Visa (apply online before departure, approximately USD 36, processed in 3 business days) or visa on arrival at Siem Reap airport (approximately USD 30, cash only). Dhesu provides pre-departure guidance on the visa process for every booking.

items.question: How do we travel from Siem Reap to Phnom Penh?
items.answer: The default transfer is by tourist express bus, which takes approximately 6 hours on the main highway south. The journey passes through the Cambodian countryside and includes a stop at Skuon Market. For travellers who prefer to fly, a domestic flight takes approximately 40 minutes. Please contact us if you would like to substitute the bus with a flight — there is an additional cost for the flight option.

items.question: Is the Royal Palace included in the tour?
items.answer: The Royal Palace is included as an exterior visit (the park and outer compound). Entrance to the inner Royal Palace complex and Silver Pagoda costs USD 10 per person and is not included in the package price — it is at your own expense if you wish to enter. The 5-day Cambodia package includes a full Royal Palace and Silver Pagoda visit as part of the Day 4 itinerary.

items.question: Is the Mekong River cruise on a private boat?
items.answer: The Mekong River sunset cruise uses shared river boats typical of the Phnom Penh riverfront. These are not exclusively private, though departure times are arranged to suit your schedule. The cruise is a local experience — you share the river with other boats, local fishing vessels, and the general river traffic of the Mekong.

items.question: Is this a private or group tour?
items.answer: The land tour arrangements (guide, vehicles, city tours) are 100% private. You have your own licensed English-speaking guide and private vehicle. The tourist express bus from Siem Reap to Phnom Penh is a shared coach service used by all tourists travelling that route.

items.question: Can halal food be arranged in Phnom Penh?
items.answer: Yes. Halal meals can be arranged in both Siem Reap and Phnom Penh at no extra cost. Phnom Penh has a number of halal restaurants. Masjid Al-Serkal in Phnom Penh can be included in your Day 4 itinerary on request.
